items.hook: On Thin Ice by Charlie Walker review – a Siberian adventure goes off the rails
items.body:
Moments of beauty and horror punctuate this account of an ill-timed sub-zero camping trip

In late February 2022, Charlie Walker set off on a hiking holiday in northern Siberia. In temperatures as low as -50C, he intended to trek a

items.hook: On the Sea review – tender fishermen’s boat-based tale of forbidden love far from prying eyes
items.body:
Away from the disapproval of their Welsh fishing community, two men embark on a passionate affair in Helen Walsh’s poignant drama

Barry Ward brings sincerity and force to this well-intended, well-acted

items.hook: Grossaktion Warsaw: The systematic deportation of Jews from the Warsaw ghetto begins.
items.body: The Grossaktion Warsaw was the Nazi code name for the deportation and mass murder of Jews from the Warsaw Ghetto during the summer of 1942, beginning on 22 July. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Salvation Boulevard is a 2011 comedy thriller film with religious satire undertones directed by George Ratliff, and starring Pierce Brosnan, Jennifer Connelly, Ed Harris, Greg Kinnear, Marisa Tomei and Jim Gaffigan.
items.body: It is based on the novel of the same name by Larry Beinhart. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: One of Those Hideous Books Where the Mother Dies (2004) is a novel in verse by Sonya Sones.
items.body: Ruby is taken away from her friends and family, all of which she loves, and is forced to cope in the fake and artificial world of Hollywood. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Magic rituals took place in an Australian cave for 25,000 years
items.body: Preserved plant cells show that grasses were brought into Cloggs cave in Australia and burnt over many millennia, revealing the ancient origins of rituals practised by the GunaiKurnai people

items.hook: 'Play music loud to remember Ozzy' says Sharon on first anniversary of his death
items.body: Sharon Osbourne shares with the BBC her memories and hopes for the future.

items.hook: The MAP75 armoured personnel carrier is a Rhodesian 4x4 heavy troop-carrying vehicle (TCV) first introduced in 1978 based on a Mercedes-Benz truck chassis.
items.body: It remains in use with the Zimbabwe National Army. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Luis Humberto Crosthwaite is a writer, editor, and journalist whose work has appeared in a variety of international venues.
items.body: His fiction works have garnered critical attention for his ability to express the complexities of living on the US/Mexico border region. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Rebel Wilson cleared in defamation case brought by actress in her own film
items.body: Wilson was accused of defaming young Australian actress Charlotte MacInnes, but cleared by a Sydney court.

items.hook: Gruesome images in D4vd hearing are 'worst nightmare' for teen girl's family
items.body: The court hears the singer ordered a body bag, chainsaws and an inflatable pool to dispose of the teen's body.

items.hook: Judge rules Trump must hand over records sought by BBC in defamation case
items.body: The judge grants a motion to compel the release of files held by the Donald J Trump Revocable Trust.

items.hook: Battle of St. Jakob an der Sihl in the Old Zürich War.
items.body: The Battle of St. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Meu Pé de Laranja Lima is a novel by José Mauro de Vasconcelos.
items.body: The book was first published in 1968 and was used for literature classes for elementary schools in Brazil. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Eurico, the Presbyter is an 1844 historical novel by Alexandre Herculano.
items.body: Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: A coalition of north-east German towns, counts and princes defeats King Valdemar II of Denmark in the Battle of Bornhöved.
items.body: Valdemar II Valdemarsen, later remembered as Valdemar the Victorious and Valdemar the Conqueror, was King of Denmark from 1202 until his death in 1241. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Mariner program: Mariner 1 spacecraft flies erratically several minutes after launch and has to be destroyed.
items.body: The Mariner program was conducted by the American space agency NASA to explore other planets. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Martial law in Poland is officially revoked.
items.body: Martial law in Poland existed between 13 December 1981 and 22 July 1983. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: First Crusade: Godfrey of Bouillon is elected the first Defender of the Holy Sepulchre of The Kingdom of Jerusalem.
items.body: The First Crusade (1096–1099) was the first of a series of religious wars, or Crusades, which were initiated, supported and at times directed by the Latin Church in the Middle Ages. The world is stranger than it lets on.
